title: lepidocoma nilgheriensis
intro: Lepidocoma nilgheriensis, also known as Nilgiri bush or Nilgiri shrub, is a species of flowering plant in the family Thymelaeaceae. It is native to the Western Ghats mountain range in India and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating wounds, and relieving digestive issues. Lepidocoma nilgheriensis is often used in herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
